editableGoldMemories.label: Preferences/Interaction_Preferences
editableGoldMemories.value: Prefers text modifications for clarity: fewer words, easier readability, splitting sentences
editableGoldMemories.reasoning: User made task-local editing requests (rewriting, simplification, sentence splitting) but no explicit self-report of stable preference. Could be transient task context.

evidence.text: Rewrite the following sentence with 20 percent fewer words...

editableNewDialogue.text: Make this sentence clearer: The committee reviewed the proposal, but because the budget was uncertain, they postponed a vote until next quarter.
The committee reviewed the proposal. Because the budget was uncertain, it postponed the vote until next quarter.
Rewrite with fewer words: A sudden gust of wind scattered the papers across the hallway, forcing everyone to stop and help pick them up.
A sudden gust scattered papers down the hallway, forcing everyone to stop and pick them up.
Split into two sentences: The city council approved the measure after hours of debate, though several members still had concerns.
The city council approved the measure after hours of debate. Several members still had concerns.
Make this easier to read: In 1977, NASA launched Voyager 1, which later became the farthest human-made object from Earth.
NASA launched Voyager 1 in 1977. It later became the farthest human-made object from Earth.

Can you tighten this without changing the meaning? I need it to fit on a slide before my civics class: “A constitutional amendment may be proposed by a two-thirds vote in both the House and the Senate.”
A constitutional amendment can be proposed with a two-thirds vote in the House and Senate.
Actually, don’t rewrite it for clarity or shorten it. Keep my original wording exactly as-is; I’m not looking for easier readability or splitting sentences this time.
Got it—I'll leave the text unchanged, with no shortening, simplification, or sentence splitting.

editableUpdatedMemories.label: Preferences/Interaction_Preferences
editableUpdatedMemories.reasoning: The prior memory inferred a stable preference for clarity edits (shortening/simplifying/splitting). The user explicitly contradicts that here by requesting no rewriting and to keep the original wording unchanged, indicating they do not want those modifications in this context.
editableUpdatedMemories.value: Does not want text rewritten/shortened for clarity and may prefer keeping original wording unchanged (at least in some requests).
